name: Chicken Thighs with Aromatic Spices
recipeIngredient: 4 Long banana peppers, cut into long strips
2 tbsp Cider vinegar or to taste
recipeInstructions:
1. Bring 7 cups water to a boil in a large saucepan. Add chicken thighs and a little more water to cover if needed.
2. Skim off any grayish foam that rises during the first few minutes of simmering.
3. Add black pepper, cumin, oregano, bay leaves, 1 tsp salt, and garlic.
4. Partially cover and simmer gently for 20 to 25 minutes, until the juices run clear when the thighs are pierced with a knife (boned thighs will cook in 15 to 20 minutes).
5. Remove from the heat and cool the chicken in the cooking liquid, if time permits. With a slotted spoon, remove thighs from the broth and set them on a plate.
6. Strain the broth, skim fat, and set aside 2 1/2 cups in the refrigerator.
7. Rub 1 tbsp Mixed Spice Paste over the side of the chicken that has been skinned and let it stand 1 hour uncovered in the refrigerator.
8. Lightly dust the spice-covered side of the chicken with flour, patting gently to evenly distribute it over the surface and remove any excess.
9. Rinse onions, drain thoroughly, and set aside with chilies.
10. Set a large nonstick skillet over medium heat, add oil, and when hot, add chicken, spice-side down, and fry until crisp, about 4 to 5 minutes. Drain on paper towels and keep warm in a 200°F oven.
11. Add onions and chilies. Cook over medium for 4 to 5 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the onions soften.
12. Add vinegar, reserved broth, and remaining 1/2 tbsp spice paste, stirring to dissolve the paste. Simmer for several minutes to blend the flavors. Taste for salt and vinegar.
13. Place 2 thighs in each of 4 bowls. Top with a portion of the onion mixture and the broth.
Calories per serving: 234
Protein: 30 G
Fat: 7 G
Carbohydrates: 13 G
Sodium: 208 MG
Cholesterol: 77 MG
